The model's baseline forecast favors Spain at 71%, leaving Uruguay with a modest 8% chance of victory. Spain's dominance in the win probability reflects a substantial gap between the two teams in the model's assessment. Uruguay's path to three points is therefore a clear underdog scenario within this match.

The draw emerges as a meaningful outcome at 21%, suggesting the model rates this fixture as genuinely competitive around a full-time result. The win probabilities separate meaningfully—Spain ahead by roughly 11 percentage points—yet the draw probability indicates the margin could compress through ninety minutes. A one-goal result or stalemate are plausible terminal states.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge noticeably. Books price Spain 11 percentage points lower than the model, and Uruguay 10 points higher, implying materially greater faith in the underdog's chances. Polymarket sits between the two, closer to the sportsbook view. The dashboard does not adjudicate which reading is correct, only that material disagreement exists across sources on this matchup.
